key_points: A triangle is a polygon with three sides and three angles
Sum of interior angles of a triangle is 180°
Types of triangles based on sides: equilateral, isosceles, scalene
Types of triangles based on angles: acute, right, obtuse
Pythagoras theorem applies to right-angled triangles
Triangles are congruent if they satisfy SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS conditions
examples: Equilateral triangle: all sides equal, all angles 60°
Isosceles triangle: two sides equal, two angles equal
Scalene triangle: all sides and angles unequal
Right triangle with sides 3,4,5 satisfies Pythagoras theorem
exam_tips: Check for congruence conditions before proving triangles are equal
Use Pythagoras theorem for right triangles only
Always label sides and angles clearly in diagrams
Sum of angles in triangle is always 180°, useful for finding unknown angles
For Heron’s formula, remember semi-perimeter s = (a+b+c)/2
